facts.title: Low tire pressure increases fuel consumption
facts.hook: Your car feels fine, but fuel runs out faster?
facts.fact: Underinflated tires increase rolling resistance, forcing the engine to work harder.
lines: Low pressure creates more friction on the road.
The engine needs more power to move the car.
Check tire pressure at least once a month.
facts.cta: When was the last time you checked your tires?

bg.query: car tire pressure check close up realistic photo

facts.title: Dirty air filters reduce engine performance
facts.hook: Does your car feel weaker than usual?
facts.fact: A clogged air filter restricts airflow needed for proper combustion.
lines: Replacing air filters is cheap but impactful.
facts.cta: Is your air filter still clean?

bg.query: dirty car air filter realistic photo

facts.title: Old engine oil loses its lubricating ability
facts.hook: Rarely drive your car? Oil still matters!
facts.fact: Engine oil degrades over time even if the car is rarely used.
lines: Change oil based on time, not just mileage.
facts.cta: When did you last change your oil?

bg.query: engine oil dipstick check realistic photo

facts.title: Driving with low fuel can damage the fuel pump
facts.hook: Do you wait until the fuel light turns on?
facts.fact: Fuel pumps rely on gasoline for cooling and lubrication.
lines: Low fuel causes the pump to overheat.
Sediment can be sucked into the system.
Refuel before the tank gets too low.
facts.cta: Do you often drive on empty?

bg.query: low fuel warning car dashboard realistic photo

facts.title: Worn shock absorbers wear tires unevenly
facts.hook: New tires but already worn?
facts.fact: Weak shock absorbers prevent tires from maintaining proper road contact.
facts.cta: Have your shocks been inspected?

bg.query: car shock absorber leak realistic photo

facts.title: Running the AC reduces engine power
facts.hook: Why does the car feel slower with AC on?
facts.fact: The AC compressor draws power directly from the engine.
lines: Small engines feel it the most.
facts.cta: Do you notice the power drop?

bg.query: car air conditioning compressor engine realistic photo

facts.title: Weak batteries affect fuel efficiency
facts.hook: A battery problem can waste fuel?
facts.fact: A weak battery forces the alternator to work harder, increasing engine load.
lines: Alternator draws power from the engine.
facts.cta: Is your battery still healthy?

bg.query: car battery inspection realistic photo

facts.title: Bent wheels cause steering vibrations
facts.hook: Steering wheel shaking at speed?
facts.fact: Bent rims disrupt wheel balance and rotation.
lines: Vibrations travel to the steering wheel.
facts.cta: Have you hit a pothole recently?

bg.query: bent car wheel rim realistic photo

facts.title: Overheated brakes lose stopping power
facts.hook: Ever felt the brakes go soft?
facts.fact: Excessive heat reduces brake friction, a condition called brake fade.
facts.cta: Have you experienced brake fade?

bg.query: car brake disc overheating realistic photo

facts.title: Windshield wipers affect driving safety
facts.hook: Rainy drive but visibility is poor?
facts.fact: Worn wiper blades leave water streaks on the windshield.
lines: Replace wipers at least once a year.
facts.cta: Are your wipers still flexible?

bg.query: car windshield wiper close up realistic photo

facts.title: Misaligned wheels make tires wear faster
facts.hook: Does your car pull to one side?
facts.fact: Poor wheel alignment causes uneven tire wear and unstable handling.
lines: One side of the tire wears quicker.
facts.cta: When did you last do wheel alignment?

bg.query: car wheel alignment service realistic photo

facts.title: Over-tightening lug nuts can warp brake rotors
facts.hook: DIY tire change? Be careful!
facts.fact: Uneven or excessive torque on lug nuts can distort brake rotors over time.
lines: Use a torque wrench for consistency.
facts.cta: Do you torque your lug nuts correctly?

bg.query: torque wrench tightening car wheel lug nuts realistic photo

facts.title: Short trips are harder on engines than long drives
facts.hook: Only driving 5 minutes each time?
facts.fact: Frequent short trips prevent the engine from reaching proper operating temperature.
lines: Moisture and fuel dilution build up in oil.
Exhaust systems don’t dry out fully.
Occasional longer drives help a lot!
facts.cta: Are your drives mostly short trips?

bg.query: city traffic short trip car realistic photo

facts.title: A loose gas cap can trigger a check engine light
facts.hook: Check engine light for no reason?
facts.fact: A loose or damaged fuel cap can cause an EVAP leak detection fault.
lines: Fuel vapors escape from the tank.
The EVAP system detects pressure loss.
Tighten the cap until it clicks!
facts.cta: Have you checked your gas cap lately?

bg.query: car fuel cap close up realistic photo

facts.title: Dirty throttle bodies can cause rough idle
facts.hook: Idle shaking at stoplights?
facts.fact: Carbon buildup in the throttle body disrupts airflow control at low RPM.
facts.cta: Have you ever cleaned your throttle body?

bg.query: car throttle body carbon buildup realistic photo

facts.title: Old spark plugs can silently waste fuel
facts.hook: Fuel economy dropping slowly over time?
facts.fact: Worn spark plugs weaken ignition, reducing combustion efficiency.
lines: Misfires may be subtle at first.
facts.cta: When were your spark plugs replaced?

bg.query: spark plugs worn vs new realistic photo

facts.title: Brake pads can be “fine” but still unsafe
facts.hook: Pads look thick, so it’s safe?
facts.fact: Brake pads can glaze or crack, reducing friction even with decent thickness.
lines: Inspect pad surface, not just thickness!
facts.cta: Do you check pad condition or just thickness?

bg.query: car brake pads inspection close up realistic photo

facts.title: A dirty cabin filter weakens AC airflow
facts.hook: AC cold but airflow feels weak?
facts.fact: A clogged cabin filter restricts air entering the cabin vents.
facts.cta: Have you changed your cabin filter yet?

bg.query: dirty cabin air filter car realistic photo

facts.title: Using the wrong engine oil viscosity can increase wear
facts.hook: Oil is oil, right? Not always!
facts.fact: Incorrect viscosity can reduce protection at cold starts or high temperatures.
lines: Too thick may starve parts when cold.
Too thin may fail under heat and load.
facts.cta: Do you use the recommended oil grade?

bg.query: engine oil bottles viscosity labels realistic photo

facts.title: Overfilling engine oil can be harmful
facts.hook: More oil equals better protection? Nope!
facts.fact: Too much oil can cause foaming and raise crankcase pressure.
lines: Keep oil level within the dipstick marks!
facts.cta: Have you ever overfilled your oil?

bg.query: car engine oil dipstick overfill realistic photo

facts.title: Dirty radiator fins reduce cooling efficiency
facts.hook: Overheating in traffic for no reason?
facts.fact: Dust and bugs clog radiator fins, reducing airflow and heat transfer.
facts.cta: Have you checked your radiator fins?

bg.query: car radiator fins dirty bugs realistic photo

facts.title: Aging rubber hoses can fail without warning
facts.hook: Hoses look okay… until they burst!
facts.fact: Rubber hoses harden and crack over time due to heat cycles and chemicals.
lines: Inspect hoses for softness and cracks!
facts.cta: When did you last inspect your hoses?

bg.query: car engine rubber hose inspection realistic photo

facts.title: Exhaust leaks can reduce performance and raise cabin risk
facts.hook: Exhaust sounds louder than usual?
facts.fact: Leaks can confuse oxygen sensor readings and may allow fumes to enter the cabin.
lines: Air-fuel mixture can become less accurate.
facts.cta: Have you noticed a new exhaust smell?

bg.query: car exhaust leak inspection realistic photo

facts.title: Headlights can be dim due to bad grounds
facts.hook: New bulbs but still dim lights?
facts.fact: Corroded grounds or connectors cause voltage drop to the headlights.
facts.cta: Have you checked your headlight wiring?

bg.query: car headlight wiring corrosion realistic photo

facts.title: Old engine mounts can stress exhaust and drivetrain parts
facts.hook: Feeling extra vibration in the cabin?
facts.fact: Worn mounts allow excessive engine movement, stressing connected components.
facts.cta: Do you feel new vibrations lately?

bg.query: car engine mount worn realistic photo

facts.title: Tire age matters even if tread looks good
facts.hook: Tread is thick, so it’s safe? Maybe not!
facts.fact: Rubber hardens with age, reducing grip even if tread depth is fine.
facts.cta: Do you know how old your tires are?

bg.query: tire DOT date code close up realistic photo

facts.title: Overloading your car increases heat and wear
facts.hook: Car packed heavy for a trip?
facts.fact: Excess weight increases braking distance, tire heat, and suspension stress.
lines: Tires heat up and wear faster.
facts.cta: Do you know your car’s load limit?

bg.query: car overloaded luggage suspension realistic photo

facts.title: Skipping car washes can accelerate paint damage
facts.hook: Not washing your car saves time… but costs paint!
facts.fact: Road salt, bird droppings, and grime can eat into clear coat and paint.
facts.cta: When did you last wash your car?

bg.query: car paint damage bird droppings realistic photo

facts.title: Parking with the wheels turned can stress steering parts
facts.hook: Always leaving your wheels fully turned?
facts.fact: Keeping steering at full lock for long periods can strain components in some systems.
lines: Power steering systems can hold extra load.
Tires can develop slight flat spots.
Straighten wheels when parking if possible!
facts.cta: Do you park with wheels turned often?

bg.query: car parked with wheels turned full lock realistic photo
